segment.source: Do not use first-letter navigation in the folder tree view: this option is handy if you use the "Quick folder key navigation" add-on;
segment.target: Do not use first-letter navigation in the folder tree view: this option is handy if you use the "Quick folder key navigation" add-on;

segment.source: Indirect initials navigation: Displays a folder name edit box as soon as you press a letter or number in the folder tree view. If this option is disabled, you can display this search box by pressing letter z;
segment.target: Indirect initials navigation: Displays a folder name edit box as soon as you press a letter or number in the folder tree view. If this option is disabled, you can display this search box by pressing letter z;

segment.source: Ignore receipt requests: If enabled, the receipt request pane will be dismissed when a message is selected in the message list;
segment.target: Ignore receipt requests: If enabled, the receipt request pane will be dismissed when a message is selected in the message list;

segment.source: Do not emulate Shift+F6 with escape: disables the use of the escape key when navigating panels in the main window;
segment.target: Do not emulate Shift+F6 with escape: disables the use of the escape key when navigating panels in the main window;

segment.source: Show only folders containing unread in the 'Tree view folders' dialog box a: displays only folders with unread messages each time you open this dialog via the F12 key;
segment.target: Show only folders containing unread in the 'Tree view folders' dialog box a: displays only folders with unread messages each time you open this dialog via the F12 key;

segment.source: Separate reading window: do not read the message automatically if this blocks NVDA: By default, when you open a message by pressing Enter from the message list, an uncluttered reading of the message starts when the new window opens. On some computers, if the email account is set to IMAP, NVDA may crash. When this option is checked, the automatic reading is disabled to avoid blockage.
segment.target: Separate reading window: do not read the message automatically if this blocks NVDA: By default, when you open a message by pressing Enter from the message list, an uncluttered reading of the message starts when the new window opens. On some computers, if the email account is set to IMAP, NVDA may crash. When this option is checked, the automatic reading is disabled to avoid blockage.

segment.source: If you do experience a blockage when you open this window, you can extend the delay before Automatic reading starts. To do this, press Alt+d and type a different value. If you are unable to remove this blockage, then check this option;
segment.target: If you do experience a blockage when you open this window, you can extend the delay before Automatic reading starts. To do this, press Alt+d and type a different value. If you are unable to remove this blockage, then check this option;

segment.source: Options for the write window:
segment.target: Options for the write window:

segment.source: Spell check: spell the misspelled word and suggested word: This option changes the announcements in the spell check dialog;
segment.target: Spell check: spell the misspelled word and suggested word: This option changes the announcements in the spell check dialog;

segment.source: Enable Spell Check enhancement while typing: The command for this setting does not yet work with NVDA 2022.1;
segment.target: Enable Spell Check enhancement while typing: The command for this setting does not yet work with NVDA 2022.1;

segment.source: The Escape key closes the message being composed;
segment.target: The Escape key closes the message being composed;

segment.source: Single press to display the context menu, double press to write [Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) or Shift+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) or their replacements: this option allows you to display the context menu by pressing the [Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) key which will be more practical if you very rarely type the characters [Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) and Shift+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab);
segment.target: Single press to display the context menu, double press to write [Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) or Shift+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) or their replacements: this option allows you to display the context menu by pressing the [Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) key which will be more practical if you very rarely type the characters [Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab) and Shift+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab);

segment.source: Update options:
segment.target: Update options:

segment.source: This option lets you disable Thunderbird+ automatic updates or re-enable them;
segment.target: This option lets you disable Thunderbird+ automatic updates or re-enable them;

segment.source: Deactivations for Chichi and Thunderbird+:
segment.target: Deactivations for Chichi and Thunderbird+:

segment.source: The main purpose of these options is to allow a good cohabitation between Thunderbird+ and Chichi by avoiding duplicate features. But you can use them without Chichi to suit your preferences;
segment.target: The main purpose of these options is to allow a good cohabitation between Thunderbird+ and Chichi by avoiding duplicate features. But you can use them without Chichi to suit your preferences;

segment.source: The term "folders" below refers to the folder tree view;
segment.target: The term "folders" below refers to the folder tree view;

segment.source: Folders: Space does not select the next unread message in the list and does not display the list of unread folders d: Chichi does not yet have an autonomous equivalent feature;
segment.target: Folders: Space does not select the next unread message in the list and does not display the list of unread folders d: Chichi does not yet have an autonomous equivalent feature;

segment.source: Folders: no navigation by initials d: by activating this option, Chichi will be used. This only works from top to bottom and does not announce the account name to which the activated folder belongs;
segment.target: Folders: no navigation by initials d: by activating this option, Chichi will be used. This only works from top to bottom and does not announce the account name to which the activated folder belongs;

segment.source: List messages: Space does not read the message in the preview pane l: if you check this option, it is Chichi that will speak the message in the preview pane. Unlike Thunderbird+, this reading will not be uncluttered. On the other hand, F4 and Alt+down arrow will continue to use Thunderbird+ uncluttered reading;
segment.target: List messages: Space does not read the message in the preview pane l: if you check this option, it is Chichi that will speak the message in the preview pane. Unlike Thunderbird+, this reading will not be uncluttered. On the other hand, F4 and Alt+down arrow will continue to use Thunderbird+ uncluttered reading;

segment.source: List messages: no SmartReply: disables the reply feature to a mailing list via Control+r on single press or to the sender via Control+r on double press;
segment.target: List messages: no SmartReply: disables the reply feature to a mailing list via Control+r on single press or to the sender via Control+r on double press;

segment.source: List messages: Do not handle the quick filter bar: Removes access to filtering options via down arrow;
segment.target: List messages: Do not handle the quick filter bar: Removes access to filtering options via down arrow;

segment.source: Main window: Tab does not move to the next pane: this option concerns navigation between the folder tree view, the message list and the message pane of headers;
segment.target: Main window: Tab does not move to the next pane: this option concerns navigation between the folder tree view, the message list and the message pane of headers;

segment.source: Main window: Reset the escape key to its default state. This particularly concerns the navigation between the panes mentioned above;
segment.target: Main window: Reset the escape key to its default state. This particularly concerns the navigation between the panes mentioned above;

segment.source: Allow Thunderbird to be closed with Control+w or Control+F4: by checking this option, the forementioned two shortcuts close Thunderbird when only one tab is open. This behavior is embarrassing for some people;
segment.target: Allow Thunderbird to be closed with Control+w or Control+F4: by checking this option, the forementioned two shortcuts close Thunderbird when only one tab is open. This behavior is embarrassing for some people;

segment.source: Backing up and restoring settings:
segment.target: Backing up and restoring settings:

segment.source: Back up the current configuration s: Copies the add-on's .ini file to a .inibak file.
segment.target: Back up the current configuration s: Copies the add-on's .ini file to a .inibak file.

segment.source: Restore Saved Configuration r: Copies the .inibak file to the .ini and reloads the settings;
segment.target: Restore Saved Configuration r: Copies the .inibak file to the .ini and reloads the settings;

segment.source: Reset Configuration r: Resets the add-on's default settings. Creates a backup beforehand if none already exists;
segment.target: Reset Configuration r: Resets the add-on's default settings. Creates a backup beforehand if none already exists;

segment.source: <a name="cmdMenu">
segment.target: <a name="cmdMenu">

segment.source: Commands Menu ([Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab))
segment.target: Commands Menu ([Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab))

segment.source: Choose and arrange columns from the message list: Displays the Thunderbird menu with check boxes called "Choose columns to display". When on a column name, press the right arrow to open the column layout dialog;
segment.target: Choose and arrange columns from the message list: Displays the Thunderbird menu with check boxes called "Choose columns to display". When on a column name, press the right arrow to open the column layout dialog;

segment.source: Write to the support: in French or in English. This command opens a write window pre-addressed to the add-on's support. This only works if Thunderbird is properly set as your default email client;
segment.target: Write to the support: in French or in English. This command opens a write window pre-addressed to the add-on's support. This only works if Thunderbird is properly set as your default email client;

segment.source: <a name="startup">
segment.target: <a name="startup">

segment.source: Enhanced Thunderbird startup
segment.target: Enhanced Thunderbird startup

segment.source: Natively, after Thunderbird's last closes, it launches on the last active tab and without activating anything, which is rather unpleasant.
segment.target: Natively, after Thunderbird's last closes, it launches on the last active tab and without activating anything, which is rather unpleasant.

segment.source: To get a comfortable start, there are two add-ons that install directly on Thunderbird:
segment.target: To get a comfortable start, there are two add-ons that install directly on Thunderbird:

segment.source: Chichi, developed by Yannick: this add-on is recommended because it offers many other accessibility features and ThunderbirdPlus is designed to interact with it;
segment.target: Chichi, developed by Yannick: this add-on is recommended because it offers many other accessibility features and ThunderbirdPlus is designed to interact with it;

segment.source: Start with inbox: if you don't use Chichi, this module does a good job;
segment.target: Start with inbox: if you don't use Chichi, this module does a good job;

segment.source: <a name="chichi">
segment.target: <a name="chichi">

segment.source: With Chichi
segment.target: With Chichi

segment.source: To use Chichi, read the Download and Installation section together with the Set the Startup Folder section of the [Chichi WebPage](https://www.rptools.org/Outils-DV/thunderbird-chichi-en.html);
segment.target: To use Chichi, read the Download and Installation section together with the Set the Startup Folder section of the [Chichi WebPage](https://www.rptools.org/Outils-DV/thunderbird-chichi-en.html);

segment.source: <a name="stwi">
segment.target: <a name="stwi">

segment.source: With Start with inbox
segment.target: With Start with inbox

segment.source: As of October 2022, the latest version of this Thunderbird 102 add-on was 2.5.2.
segment.target: As of October 2022, the latest version of this Thunderbird 102 add-on was 2.5.2.

segment.source: Characteristics:
segment.target: Characteristics:

segment.source: Installation: via a search by its name in the "Add-ons" tab of Thunderbird;
segment.target: Installation: via a search by its name in the "Add-ons" tab of Thunderbird;

segment.source: Startup folder: always the same, either the inbox folder of the account chosen in the add-on settings or the unified folder;
segment.target: Startup folder: always the same, either the inbox folder of the account chosen in the add-on settings or the unified folder;

segment.source: Auto-focus: on the last message in the message list or on the "Inbox" folder in the tree view, depending on the setting in the add-on options. The second option is selected by default;
segment.target: Auto-focus: on the last message in the message list or on the "Inbox" folder in the tree view, depending on the setting in the add-on options. The second option is selected by default;

segment.source: Settings: Less easy access. Go to the Add-ons tab, select "Start with inbox" in the list of installed add-ons then press enter on the "Add-on Options" button. This will open a new tab "Settings Start with inbox";
segment.target: Settings: Less easy access. Go to the Add-ons tab, select "Start with inbox" in the list of installed add-ons then press enter on the "Add-on Options" button. This will open a new tab "Settings Start with inbox";

segment.source: A drop-down list allows you to choose the email account for which the inbox folder will be selected when Thunderbird starts;
segment.target: A drop-down list allows you to choose the email account for which the inbox folder will be selected when Thunderbird starts;

segment.source: Three radio buttons will allow you to choose between focusing the last message, the first unread message in the message list or the folder in the tree view;
segment.target: Three radio buttons will allow you to choose between focusing the last message, the first unread message in the message list or the folder in the tree view;

segment.source: <a name="stwInstall">
segment.target: <a name="stwInstall">

segment.source: Installation:
segment.target: Installation:

segment.source: In Thunderbird, open the "Tools" menu and press Enter on: Add-ons and themes;
segment.target: In Thunderbird, open the "Tools" menu and press Enter on: Add-ons and themes;

segment.source: In the Add-on Manager page, go to the search field. In browse mode, you can press letter e to reach it quickly;
segment.target: In the Add-on Manager page, go to the search field. In browse mode, you can press letter e to reach it quickly;

segment.source: Write: Start with Inbox and press Enter;
segment.target: Write: Start with Inbox and press Enter;

segment.source: If you have Thunderbird+, wait a moment for it to select the add-on you are looking for in the results tab;
segment.target: If you have Thunderbird+, wait a moment for it to select the add-on you are looking for in the results tab;

segment.source: Otherwise, manually select the tab "Start with inbox:: Search:: Add-ons for Thunderbird", for instance. Next, press 3 until you reach the level 3 heading which bears the name of the add-on you have searched for;
segment.target: Otherwise, manually select the tab "Start with inbox:: Search:: Add-ons for Thunderbird", for instance. Next, press 3 until you reach the level 3 heading which bears the name of the add-on you have searched for;

segment.source: Using the down arrow, scroll down to the link "Add to Thunderbird" and press Enter on it;
segment.target: Using the down arrow, scroll down to the link "Add to Thunderbird" and press Enter on it;

segment.source: Follow the procedure then restart Thunderbird;
segment.target: Follow the procedure then restart Thunderbird;

segment.source: If all went well, Thunderbird will open on the main tab and focus the message list;
segment.target: If all went well, Thunderbird will open on the main tab and focus the message list;

segment.source: Set Start with Inbox options:
segment.target: Set Start with Inbox options:

segment.source: Return to the "Add-ons Manager" tab;
segment.target: Return to the "Add-ons Manager" tab;

segment.source: If necessary, leave the search field to be in browse mode;
segment.target: If necessary, leave the search field to be in browse mode;

segment.source: Press 3 as many times as required to reach the level 3 heading titled "Start with Inbox in the list of installed add-ons;
segment.target: Press 3 as many times as required to reach the level 3 heading titled "Start with Inbox in the list of installed add-ons;

segment.source: Next, activate the button: Add-on Options. This opens a new tab titled: Start with Inbox, Settings;
segment.target: Next, activate the button: Add-on Options. This opens a new tab titled: Start with Inbox, Settings;

segment.source: Here's what is displayed by default:
segment.target: Here's what is displayed by default:

segment.source: Start with Inbox - Settings
segment.target: Start with Inbox - Settings

segment.source: Please select the account for which its inbox shall be displayed after start of Thunderbird.
segment.target: Please select the account for which its inbox shall be displayed after start of Thunderbird.

segment.source: Drop-down list: \<your first email account\>
segment.target: Drop-down list: \<your first email account\>

segment.source: Select and put the focus on:
segment.target: Select and put the focus on:

segment.source: clickable radio button not checked: the latest* message.
segment.target: clickable radio button not checked: the latest* message.

segment.source: Clickable radio button not checked the inbox folder in the folder tree view.
segment.target: Clickable radio button not checked the inbox folder in the folder tree view.

segment.source: radio button checked: the first unread message.
segment.target: radio button checked: the first unread message.

segment.source: Definition of "latest": The last message that was put into the inbox (independent from the date of the message).
segment.target: Definition of "latest": The last message that was put into the inbox (independent from the date of the message).

segment.source: If the inbox contains no messages, the inbox folder in the folder tree will be selected and focused.
segment.target: If the inbox contains no messages, the inbox folder in the folder tree will be selected and focused.

segment.source: Start with Inbox - Réglages ;
segment.target: Start with Inbox - Réglages ;

segment.source: Sélectionnez le compte dont le dossier Courrier entrant sera affiché au démarrage de Thunderbird ;
segment.target: Sélectionnez le compte dont le dossier Courrier entrant sera affiché au démarrage de Thunderbird ;

segment.source: liste déroulante : \<votre premier compte de messagerie\>
segment.target: liste déroulante : \<votre premier compte de messagerie\>

segment.source: Sélectionner puis placer le focus sur :
segment.target: Sélectionner puis placer le focus sur :

segment.source: bouton radio cliquable non coché :Le dernier message de la liste ;
segment.target: bouton radio cliquable non coché :Le dernier message de la liste ;

segment.source: bouton radio non coché cliquable Le dossier Courrier entrant dans l'arborescence des dossiers ;
segment.target: bouton radio non coché cliquable Le dossier Courrier entrant dans l'arborescence des dossiers ;

segment.source: bouton radio coché : Le premier message non lu de la liste ;
segment.target: bouton radio coché : Le premier message non lu de la liste ;

segment.source: If the Inbox folder contains no messages, this folder will gain focus in the folder tree view;
segment.target: If the Inbox folder contains no messages, this folder will gain focus in the folder tree view;

segment.source: When you have defined your settings, restart Thunderbird.
segment.target: When you have defined your settings, restart Thunderbird.

segment.source: Built-in Thunderbird launcher via AltGr+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab):
segment.target: Built-in Thunderbird launcher via AltGr+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab):

segment.source: For convenience and speed, you can launch Thunderbird by pressing AltGr+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab).
segment.target: For convenience and speed, you can launch Thunderbird by pressing AltGr+[Grave](keyEquiv_en.html#aboveTab).

segment.source: This shortcut is configurable via NVDA's input gesture manager which offers greater freedom to choose modifier keys than Windows shortcuts, limited to AltGr;
segment.target: This shortcut is configurable via NVDA's input gesture manager which offers greater freedom to choose modifier keys than Windows shortcuts, limited to AltGr;

segment.source: Follow these steps to add another input gesture:
segment.target: Follow these steps to add another input gesture:

segment.source: First, bring the Thunderbird window to the foreground;
segment.target: First, bring the Thunderbird window to the foreground;

segment.source: Open the NVDA menu and select "Preferences";
segment.target: Open the NVDA menu and select "Preferences";

segment.source: In the submenu, press Enter on: "Input gestures";
segment.target: In the submenu, press Enter on: "Input gestures";

segment.source: In the dialog that opens press the letter t until you reach: "Thunderbird, Launcher";
segment.target: In the dialog that opens press the letter t until you reach: "Thunderbird, Launcher";

segment.source: Press the right arrow to expand this branch;
segment.target: Press the right arrow to expand this branch;

segment.source: Scroll down to the item: "Starts Thunderbird" then press right arrow to expand this level;
segment.target: Scroll down to the item: "Starts Thunderbird" then press right arrow to expand this level;

segment.source: Tab to the "Add" button, press space then press an input gesture in the new dialog;
segment.target: Tab to the "Add" button, press space then press an input gesture in the new dialog;

segment.source: Press Enter to confirm your choice;
segment.target: Press Enter to confirm your choice;

segment.source: Back in the gesture list, verify the presence of your new input gesture;
segment.target: Back in the gesture list, verify the presence of your new input gesture;

segment.source: Close the dialog with the OK button.
segment.target: Close the dialog with the OK button.

segment.source: Notes:
segment.target: Notes:

segment.source: The Thunderbird launcher is only intended for the Thunderbird installed version of which Thunderbird.exe paths are predictable.
segment.target: The Thunderbird launcher is only intended for the Thunderbird installed version of which Thunderbird.exe paths are predictable.

segment.source: In a 64-bit Windows architecture, this add-on will not launch the 32-bit version of Thunderbird if both 64-bit and 32-bit versions are installed on your system;
segment.target: In a 64-bit Windows architecture, this add-on will not launch the 32-bit version of Thunderbird if both 64-bit and 32-bit versions are installed on your system;
